title: Train a censored survival specialist for time-to-next-stop
summary: XGBoost AFT and discrete-time hazard models are designed for time-to-event problems with censoring, which is a better fit than flat binary labels for rows that reach race end without another stop.
rationale: XGBoost AFT and discrete-time hazard models are designed for time-to-event problems with censoring, which is a better fit than flat binary labels for rows that reach race end without another stop. This is a concrete, testable extension of the earlier hazard idea rather than more manual thresholding.
implementation_hint: Construct `time_to_next_pit` per row; use exact times for observed future stops and right-censor rows with no later stop. Train an `objective="survival:aft"` XGBoost model or a discrete-time hazard model, then convert the predicted event-time distribution into `P(stop on next lap)` and blend as a specialist.
expected_effect: Should especially help late-race and no-more-stop cases, where a plain classifier often overcalls because it lacks explicit censoring semantics.
risk: More complex target engineering; an AFT distribution choice can underfit if the stop-time shape is badly misspecified.
